steps.title: Read the center from standard form
steps.content: For a circle written as $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$, the center is $(h,k)$. Compare each part of the equation to that standard form.

steps.title: Recognize the standard form of a circle
steps.content: A circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ has the equation $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$. We can read the center directly from this form without any algebra.

steps.title: Extract the center from the given equation
steps.content:
The given equation is $(x+4)^2 + (y-6)^2 = 9$. Comparing to the standard form:
  • $(x+4)^2 = (x-(-4))^2$ corresponds to $(x-h)^2$, so $h = -4$
  • $(y-6)^2$ corresponds to $(y-k)^2$, so $k = 6$
Therefore, the center is $(-4, 6)$. That matches the correct answer - C is correct.
